subject: [PATCH 09/14] mm/userfaultfd: add RWP fault delivery and expose UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta)
reviews.summary: The patch author provided a detailed explanation of the RWP fault delivery mechanism and its implementation in various kernel components. They also clarified that the PROT_NONE restoration on swap-in is performed earlier in the series, addressing concerns about swapped-in RWP pages.

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for read-write protection (RWP) to the userfaultfd interface, allowing userspace to track and manage RWP-protected pages. The implementation involves modifying various kernel components, including memory management, huge page handling, and userfaultfd code. The goal is to enable efficient tracking of RWP-protected pages and provide a robust mechanism for managing these pages.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: Clarified the behavior of PROT_NONE restoration on swap-in and emphasized that VM_UFFD_WP and VM_UFFD_RWP are mutually exclusive.
reviews.raw_body:
sashiko.dev -- https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260427114607.4068647-1-kas@kernel.org -- wrote:
> Does this code lose the RWP read protection after a page is swapped out
> and back in?
> [ ... ]
> The restored PTE will be PROT_READ with the uffd bit set, instead of
> PROT_NONE. Will this silently allow subsequent read accesses to succeed
> without triggering the required RWP userfault?

The PROT_NONE restoration on swap-in is performed two commits earlier,
in patch 6/14 "mm: preserve RWP marker across PTE rewrites", which
adds to do_swap_page():

        if (pte_swp_uffd(vmf->orig_pte) && userfaultfd_rwp(vma))
                pte = pte_modify(pte, PAGE_NONE);

so a swapped-in RWP page comes back as PAGE_NONE | _PAGE_UFFD, not
PROT_READ | _PAGE_UFFD. The same patch covers unuse_pte() (the
swapoff(2) path), restore_exclusive_pte(), and the migration-entry
resolvers; each gates on userfaultfd_rwp(vma) and the swap-pte uffd
bit before re-applying PAGE_NONE.

> Can this sequence cause a state collision between RWP and NUMA-hinted
> UFFD_WP pages?
> If a VMA has both VM_UFFD_WP and VM_UFFD_RWP enabled, [ ... ]

No. VM_UFFD_WP and VM_UFFD_RWP are mutually exclusive.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch series adds read-write protection (RWP) tracking to the userfaultfd interface, allowing userspace to detect and handle RWP faults. The patch introduces a new fault delivery mechanism for RWP-protected pages and exposes UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP to enable userspace registration of RWP mode.
